description: Complete the Lalamove delivery integration so all tracking data is shown in real-time INSIDE the MASH system (not just at partnerportal.lalamove.com). Use SANDBOX credentials only. Replace 30s polling with Firestore onSnapshot subscriptions. Add seller real-time delivery panel. Full unit test coverage for all hooks and components.

sandboxCredentials.note: Keys already configured in .env — pk_test_... / sk_test_... — no production keys needed

currentState.webhookHandler: COMPLETE — src/app/api/lalamove/webhook/route.ts writes all 8 event types to Firestore
currentState.firestoreSchema: COMPLETE — lalamoveTracking subdocument in FirestoreOrder with status, driver, coordinates, timeline
currentState.trackingPagePolling: INCOMPLETE — src/app/(user)/profile/orders/[orderId]/track/page.tsx uses setInterval(30000) instead of onSnapshot
currentState.sellerRealtimePanel: MISSING — seller orders page shows Lalamove badge but no live delivery status panel
currentState.useLalamoveTrackingHook: MISSING — no dedicated hook for Firestore real-time subscription
currentState.deliveryComponents: PARTIAL — TrackingMap, StatusTimeline, PriorityDelivery, DeliveryChat components exist but have test gaps
currentState.sandboxTestRoute: MISSING — no way to trigger sandbox delivery events from within the system for demos
currentState.chatSimulation: INCOMPLETE — DeliveryChat/chat-send route has USE_TWILIO=false, simulation mode not fully wired up

successMetrics.build: npm run build passes with zero errors
successMetrics.tests: All test suites pass with 0 failures
successMetrics.lint: npm run lint passes with zero warnings
successMetrics.typecheck: npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
successMetrics.realtime: Firestore onSnapshot fires within 1s of webhook event — no 30s delay
successMetrics.testCoverage: All new files have >= 85% statement coverage

stories.title: useLalamoveTracking Real-Time Hook
stories.description: Create src/hooks/useLalamoveTracking.ts — a React hook that subscribes to a Firestore order document via onSnapshot and returns live lalamoveTracking data. Replaces all polling patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns { tracking: FirestoreOrder['lalamoveTracking'] | null, loading: boolean, error: string | null }
Uses FirebaseOrdersService subscribeToOrder (or doc onSnapshot directly) for real-time updates
Unsubscribes onSnapshot listener on component unmount (no memory leaks)
Returns loading=true until first snapshot arrives
Returns error string if Firestore read fails
Exported from src/hooks/index.ts or directly importable

stories.title: Customer Tracking Page — Replace Polling with onSnapshot
stories.description: Upgrade src/app/(user)/profile/orders/[orderId]/track/page.tsx to use the useLalamoveTracking hook instead of setInterval(30000). Updates from the Lalamove webhook should appear instantly in the UI without manual refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the setInterval 30s polling useEffect entirely
Use useLalamoveTracking(orderId) hook for live lalamoveTracking state
Driver location on TrackingMap updates automatically when Firestore changes
StatusTimeline updates automatically when delivery status changes
Retain manual Refresh button for user-triggered Lalamove API poll (fetchLalamoveUpdates still works)
Last updated timestamp shows real-time (updates on each onSnapshot event)
Page still works when order has no lalamoveOrderId (non-Lalamove orders)

stories.title: Seller Order Detail — Real-Time Lalamove Status Panel
stories.description: Add a real-time Lalamove delivery status panel to the seller orders page (src/app/(seller)/orders/firebase/page.tsx or the order detail dialog). When an order has a lalamoveOrderId, show live status, driver info, and tracking link — all updating in real-time via Firestore onSnapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: Component accepts: orderId: string, lalamoveOrderId: string, lalamoveTracking: FirestoreOrder['lalamoveTracking']
Shows: delivery status badge (ASSIGNING_DRIVER / ON_GOING / PICKED_UP / COMPLETED / CANCELED)
Shows: driver name, phone, plate number when assigned
Shows: Lalamove shareLink as clickable external link when available
Shows: StatusTimeline component with current status
PriorityDelivery component shown when status is ASSIGNING_DRIVER (can add priority fee)
Panel visible in seller order detail dialog/card for orders with deliveryMethod=lalamove
MASH brand: emerald colors, rounded-lg, shadow-sm

stories.title: Sandbox Event Simulator API Route
stories.description: Create src/app/api/lalamove/sandbox-simulate/route.ts — a POST endpoint that simulates Lalamove webhook events by writing directly to Firestore. Used to demo real-time updates in the system without needing actual sandbox deliveries. Only works when LALAMOVE_HOST contains 'sandbox'.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns 403 if LALAMOVE_HOST does not contain 'sandbox' (production safety guard)
For each event type, calls FirebaseOrdersService.updateLalamoveTracking() with realistic mock data
DRIVER_ASSIGNED: sets driver name='John Doe (Sandbox)', phone='+639171234567', plate='ABC 1234', coordinates near Manila
PICKED_UP: updates status to PICKED_UP, updates driver coordinates
COMPLETED: updates status to COMPLETED, clears driver coordinates
Returns { success: true, event, orderId, updatedAt }

stories.title: Sandbox Test Page — Full Interactive Demo
stories.description: Upgrade src/app/lalamove-test/page.tsx to be a comprehensive interactive sandbox demo page showing real-time updates flowing through the system — from 'Get Quotation' all the way to 'Delivery Completed' with live status changes visible without leaving the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 1: Quotation flow — 'Get Quote' button shows price, distance, quotationId
Section 2: Order flow — 'Place Order' button (using quotationId from Section 1), shows lalamoveOrderId
Section 3: Real-time status panel — uses useLalamoveTracking(internalOrderId) with Firestore subscription
Section 4: Sandbox simulator — buttons for each event: ASSIGNING_DRIVER, DRIVER_ASSIGNED, PICKED_UP, COMPLETED, CANCELED — calls /api/lalamove/sandbox-simulate
Section 5: Status timeline — StatusTimeline component updates live as simulator events fire
Section 6: Driver info card — shows driver details when DRIVER_ASSIGNED event fires
Section 7: Raw API data — collapsible JSON viewer showing raw Lalamove API response
Status badge animates on change (CSS transition)
All sections visible on one scrollable page
Shows [SANDBOX] banner at top so it is clearly not production

stories.title: DeliveryChat Sandbox Simulation Mode
stories.description: Complete src/components/delivery/DeliveryChat.tsx and src/app/api/lalamove/chat/send/route.ts simulation mode. Since USE_TWILIO=false in sandbox, messages should be stored in Firestore under orders/{orderId}/chatMessages subcollection and displayed in real-time via onSnapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: chat/send POST stores message in Firestore: orders/{orderId}/chatMessages/{messageId}
chat/send GET reads chat history from Firestore subcollection (not an in-memory store)
Message document schema: { id, sender, message, timestamp, status: 'sent' | 'delivered' }
DeliveryChat component uses Firestore onSnapshot on chatMessages subcollection for real-time new messages
Sandbox simulation: after 3 seconds, auto-reply driver message: 'On my way! (Sandbox auto-reply)'
Error state shown when send fails

stories.title: Unit Tests — useLalamoveTracking Hook
stories.description: Write complete unit tests for src/hooks/useLalamoveTracking.ts covering all states and edge cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests use jest.mock for Firebase Firestore (mock onSnapshot)
Test: initial state is loading=true, tracking=null
Test: after first snapshot, loading=false, tracking=data
Test: when snapshot updates (status change), tracking updates in hook
Test: when orderId is undefined/null, hook returns loading=false, tracking=null
Test: unsubscribe function is called on unmount (no memory leak)
Test: when Firestore throws error, error state is set
Test: when order has no lalamoveTracking field, tracking=null

stories.title: Unit Tests — Delivery UI Components
stories.description: Write/complete unit tests for all delivery components: TrackingMap, StatusTimeline, PriorityDelivery, DeliveryChat, SellerDeliveryPanel.
acceptanceCriteria: StatusTimeline tests: renders all 4 stages, marks correct stage as current, shows CANCELED state, shows COMPLETED state
PriorityDelivery tests: renders options, calls onPrioritySelected with correct fee, shows loading on API call, handles error
DeliveryChat tests: renders chat history, send button calls /api/lalamove/chat/send, quick replies populate input, shows error on send failure
SellerDeliveryPanel tests: shows ASSIGNING_DRIVER state, shows driver info when DRIVER_ASSIGNED, shows completed state, shows shareLink
All new tests mock Firebase, next/navigation, and fetch
Minimum 30 total test cases across all files

stories.title: Unit Tests — Sandbox Simulate Route + Test Page
stories.description: Write unit tests for the sandbox simulator API route and the lalamove-test demo page.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulator route tests: returns 403 when not sandbox, accepts all 5 event types, calls updateLalamoveTracking with correct data for each event, returns success response

Test page: renders all 6 sections, Get Quote button calls /api/lalamove/quotation, Place Order button calls /api/lalamove/order, simulator buttons call /api/lalamove/sandbox-simulate, rawData section toggles
All tests mock Firebase, Lalamove client, and fetch

stories.title: Unit Tests — Customer Tracking Page Real-Time
stories.description: Update/write tests for the customer tracking page to verify real-time Firestore subscription behavior (not polling).
acceptanceCriteria: Test: onSnapshot is called (not setInterval) for real-time updates
Test: UI updates when Firestore tracking data changes (simulated snapshot update)
Test: LoadingMap and StatusTimeline receive updated props when snapshot changes
Test: manual Refresh button still works (calls fetchLalamoveUpdates)
Test: page shows 'no delivery tracking' for non-Lalamove orders
Test: verifies no setInterval calls remain

stories.title: Webhook Signature Verification — Always-On with Timing-Safe Compare
stories.description: Harden the webhook route: replace naive string equality with crypto.timingSafeEqual, enforce signature verification in ALL environments (sandbox included), and add request body size limit. Currently sandbox skips verification entirely, which masks bugs.
acceptanceCriteria: verifyWebhookSignature uses crypto.timingSafeEqual instead of === (prevents timing attacks)
Signature verification runs in BOTH sandbox and production (remove isSandbox skip)
If X-Lalamove-Signature header is missing, return 401 with { success: false, message: 'Missing signature' }
If signature mismatch, return 401 with { success: false, message: 'Invalid signature' }
Request body limited to 1MB (return 413 if exceeded)
rawBody parsed with try/catch — malformed JSON returns 400 not 500
All existing event handlers remain unchanged

stories.title: Delivery ETA Countdown Timer Component
stories.description: Create a countdown timer component that shows estimated time remaining for delivery. Reads eta.minutes from lalamoveTracking and decrements live. Used in both customer tracking page and seller delivery panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts eta: { minutes: number, distance: number } | null prop
Shows 'Estimated: Xm remaining' when eta is provided
Timer decrements every 60 seconds (not every second — minutes granularity)
Shows distance in km: '3.2 km away'
Stops countdown at 0 (does not go negative)
Uses emerald/green color scheme matching MASH brand
Test environment guard: timer disabled when typeof jest !== 'undefined'

stories.title: Delivery Cost Calculator Widget
stories.description: Create a standalone delivery cost calculator widget that lets users get a Lalamove quote by entering pickup and delivery addresses. Uses the existing /api/lalamove/quotation endpoint. Can be embedded in checkout or shown as a standalone tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Two address inputs: pickup and delivery (text inputs with lat/lng defaults for Manila)
Vehicle type selector using existing vehicle-types.ts data (7 types with icons)
Shows price breakdown: base, extra mileage, total
Shows distance and estimated delivery info
Loading state with skeleton while fetching quote
Error state with retry button on API failure
onQuoteReceived callback prop for parent integration
MASH brand styling: emerald accents, rounded-lg card, shadow-sm
Mobile responsive — stacks inputs vertically on < 640px

stories.title: Order-to-Delivery Automation — createLalamoveOrder on Seller Approval
stories.description: Wire the seller approval flow so that when a seller approves an order with deliveryMethod=lalamove, the system automatically calls /api/lalamove/create-order to place the Lalamove delivery. Currently this is manual. Add error handling and status rollback on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: When seller approves order with deliveryMethod=lalamove, auto-call create-order endpoint
Create /api/orders/approve route if it does not exist
Route accepts POST { orderId, sellerId } and calls FirebaseOrdersService.approveOrder
After approval, if deliveryMethod=lalamove AND lalamoveQuotationId exists, call /api/lalamove/create-order internally
On success: update order with lalamoveOrderId and set lalamoveTracking.status=ASSIGNING_DRIVER
On failure: keep order approved but log error and set deliveryStatus='failed_to_create'
Return { success: true, lalamoveOrderId } or { success: true, deliveryError: string }

stories.title: Mobile-Optimized Tracking Bottom Sheet
stories.description: Create a mobile-optimized bottom sheet component for the tracking page that slides up from the bottom on mobile viewports. Shows delivery status, driver info, and ETA in a compact format. On desktop, falls back to the existing side panel layout.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (< 768px): renders as bottom sheet with drag handle
Three states: collapsed (shows status + ETA), half (adds driver info + timeline), full (adds chat)
Swipe up/down to change states (CSS transform, no heavy library)
On desktop (>= 768px): renders as standard Card component (no sheet behavior)
Uses useLalamoveTracking hook for real-time data
Shows ETACountdown component when eta data available
Shows driver call button when driver assigned
Handles touch events for swipe gesture
MASH brand: emerald accent, rounded-t-2xl on mobile

stories.title: Unit Tests — Webhook Route Hardening
stories.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for the hardened webhook route (LAMA-011). Test signature verification, body size limits, malformed JSON, and all 8 event handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: timing-safe compare is used (mock crypto.timingSafeEqual)
Test: all 8 event types dispatched correctly
Test: unknown event type returns 200 (ack but no-op)
Test: findOrderByLalamoveId returns null when no match
Test: Firestore updateLalamoveTracking called with correct data per event

stories.title: TrackingMap Fallback — Static Map When No Google API Key
stories.description: The current TrackingMap requires a Google Maps API key. Create a fallback static map component that shows pickup/dropoff/driver locations using CSS-positioned markers on a simple grid when NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is not set. This allows the tracking page to work in all environments.
acceptanceCriteria: StaticTrackingMap renders a visual representation with positioned markers
Shows pickup marker (green, labeled A), dropoff marker (red, labeled B)
Shows driver marker (blue, animated pulse) when driverLocation provided
Dashed line connecting pickup to dropoff
Shows coordinates text below each marker
TrackingMap auto-falls back to StaticTrackingMap when NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is missing
Both components accept same props interface
Responsive: full width, 300px height on mobile, 400px on desktop

stories.title: Delivery Status Change Toast Notifications
stories.description: Add toast notifications that fire when the Lalamove tracking status changes. Uses the existing useLalamoveTracking hook to detect changes and shows a sonner toast with status-specific messaging and icons.
acceptanceCriteria: Hook accepts tracking data from useLalamoveTracking
Fires toast.info on ASSIGNING_DRIVER: 'Finding a driver for your delivery...'
Fires toast.success on DRIVER_ASSIGNED (ON_GOING): 'Driver {name} assigned! Plate: {plate}'
Fires toast.info on PICKED_UP: 'Your order has been picked up!'
Fires toast.success on COMPLETED: 'Delivery complete! Your order has arrived.'
Fires toast.error on CANCELED: 'Delivery has been canceled.'
Uses useRef to track previous status — only fires on actual change (not on mount)
Uses sonner toast from existing project dependency
Test environment guard: no toasts when typeof jest !== 'undefined'
